records.event.description: S-3 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.description: S-282 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-275 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-1 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-262 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-283 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-4 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy) is a package rbac that implements the authorizer.Authorizer interface using roles base access control.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass. Endpoint & EndpointSlice permissions allow cross-Namespace forwarding. If a potential attacker can create or edit Endpoints or EndpointSlices in the Kubernetes API, they can potentially direct a LoadBalancer or Ingress implementation to expose backend IPs the attacker should not have access to.

records.event.description: S-2 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-1 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-262 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-283 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-3 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.description: S-1 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-262 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-283 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'` - **Affected compnents entities id:   - **Affected components name:  - **Affected Entities**: KUBERNETES_NODE-76E20DB8A58FDAF4 - **Technology **: KUBERNETES ## Instructions Please: 1. Explain what this vulnerability means in simple terms. 2. Describe the potential impact on the system. 3. Provide detailed remediation steps (including code/config changes if relevant). 4. Suggest best practices to prevent similar issues in the future.

records.event.description: S-286 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.event.description: S-4 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy) is a package rbac that implements the authorizer.Authorizer interface using roles base access control.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass. Endpoint & EndpointSlice permissions allow cross-Namespace forwarding. If a potential attacker can create or edit Endpoints or EndpointSlices in the Kubernetes API, they can potentially direct a LoadBalancer or Ingress implementation to expose backend IPs the attacker should not have access to.

records.event.description: S-284 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`

records.event.description: S-2 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-263 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
``` - **Affected compnents entities id:   - **Affected components name:  - **Affected Entities**: KUBERNETES_NODE-AE5EB9C06027393F - **Technology **: KUBERNETES ## Instructions Please: 1. records.event.description: S-274 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-285 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.event.description: S-3 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.description: S-280 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-274 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-2 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-4 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy) is a package rbac that implements the authorizer.Authorizer interface using roles base access control.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass. Endpoint & EndpointSlice permissions allow cross-Namespace forwarding. If a potential attacker can create or edit Endpoints or EndpointSlices in the Kubernetes API, they can potentially direct a LoadBalancer or Ingress implementation to expose backend IPs the attacker should not have access to.

records.event.description: S-262 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-3 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.description: S-275 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-284 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-263 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-280 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-285 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.event.description: S-274 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-3 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.description: S-2 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-4 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy) is a package rbac that implements the authorizer.Authorizer interface using roles base access control.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass. Endpoint & EndpointSlice permissions allow cross-Namespace forwarding. If a potential attacker can create or edit Endpoints or EndpointSlices in the Kubernetes API, they can potentially direct a LoadBalancer or Ingress implementation to expose backend IPs the attacker should not have access to.

records.event.description: S-262 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-275 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-282 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-286 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.event.description: S-284 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-1 Open Redirect state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io) is a Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Open Redirect. An actor that controls the responses of `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` or `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` requests is able to redirect `kube-apiserver` requests to private networks of the `apiserver`. If that user can view `kube-apiserver` logs when the log level is set to 10, they can view the redirected responses and headers in the logs.

This vulnerability requires the `-v` log level to be set to a minimum of 10 and `--profiling` to be enabled on the `kube-apiserver` to be exploitable.

records.event.description: S-280 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-274 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-4 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/plugin/pkg/auth/authorizer/rbac/bootstrappolicy) is a package rbac that implements the authorizer.Authorizer interface using roles base access control.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass. Endpoint & EndpointSlice permissions allow cross-Namespace forwarding. If a potential attacker can create or edit Endpoints or EndpointSlices in the Kubernetes API, they can potentially direct a LoadBalancer or Ingress implementation to expose backend IPs the attacker should not have access to.

records.event.description: S-262 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-285 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description: Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the `NodeRestriction` admission controller. An attacker can cause unauthorized deletion of node objects by patching them with an `OwnerReference` to a cluster-scoped resource, resulting in the node being deleted through garbage collection if the referenced resource does not exist or is later removed.

records.event.description: S-3 Access Restriction Bypass state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
[k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg) is a Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called Borg, combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Access Restriction Bypass when performing a second DNS resolution without validation for the actual connection.
If a non-standard DNS server returns different non-cached responses, a user may be able to bypass the proxy IP restriction and access private networks on the control plane.

records.event.description: S-275 Race Condition state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Race Condition during the namespace deletion process in `deleteAllContent()` in `namespaced_resources_deleter.go`. An attacker can bypass network restrictions because network policies are deleted before the pods they are meant to protect.

All clusters using the Kubernetes NetworkPolicy API may be vulnerable.

records.event.description: S-284 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

records.event.description: S-263 Improper Input Validation state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Input Validation. An attacker with create pod permission could access local git repositories belonging to other pods on the same node by exploiting this vulnerability.

**Notes:**

1) This is only exploitable if the cluster still uses the deprecated in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature to clone git repositories from other pods within the same node;

2) The in-tree `gitRepo` volume feature has been deprecated and will not receive security updates upstream;

3) To detect whether this vulnerability has been exploited, the following command can be used to list all pods that use the in-tree `gitRepo` volume and clone to a `.git` subdirectory:

```go
`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository | test("^/")) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, repository: (.spec.volumes[] | select(.gitRepo) | .gitRepo.repository)}'`
```

records.event.description: S-280 Incorrect Authorization state event reported
records.event.name: Vulnerability historical state report event
records.vulnerability.description:
Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Incorrect Authorization via the NodeRestriction admission controller process. An attacker can access unauthorized dynamic resources by creating mirror pods during pod creation when the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate is enabled.

**Note:**

The `DynamicResourceAllocation` feature is disabled by default but it is recommended to check if it is enabled and to turn it off if not actively used.

To check if the feature is in use run the following commands:

- `kubectl get ResourceClaim --all-namespaces`
- `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations["kubernetes.io/config.mirror"] == "true")  | "\(.metadata.namespace)/(.metadata.name)"'`

sections.markdown:
##### Exercise 6.4

Now that we have our template complete with dynamic data, let's conduct some CoPilot searches :

* In the returned data set from the prior example, find the `prompt` column and select one of the returned rows 
* Click on the cell and a `...` will appear in the top right corner of the cell
* Click `Open With` and select  `Dynatrace copilot`.
* Dynatrace will now return a secured LLM analysis in response to your investigation on the Vulnerability you selected.
